message:
Last weekend my sister called in a panic—power was out in her neighborhood, and she couldn't find her insurance documents or the kids' medical info. I felt this weird moment of... relief? Because I'd actually *organized* mine six months ago. Found everything in about 30 seconds and texted her photos. 

For *once* I was the calm, prepared one instead of the person improvising. It wasn't about being perfect—just knowing where my stuff was. Even she seemed genuinely relieved, and honestly, that felt better than any amount of scrambling.

I think that's the real win with home readiness: not paranoia, just the quiet confidence of knowing you've got your family's basics covered when life gets messy.

Have you got your important documents somewhere you can actually *find* them when you need them?
